exclusion_rules.description: RKSK services target adolescents aged 10 to 19 years. Children below 10 are covered under child-health programmes (e.g. RBSK) and those above 19 under general adult health services.

family_criteria.family_definition: Universal adolescent health programme — every adolescent aged 10-19 years is eligible regardless of family income, caste, religion, or BPL status: boys and girls, rural and urban, married and unmarried, in-school and out-of-school, with special focus on marginalised and underserved groups. Services are free at Adolescent Friendly Health Clinics and through community outreach.

covered_procedures: Free preventive, promotive, curative and counselling services for adolescents across six priority areas
Nutrition: Weekly Iron and Folic Acid Supplementation (WIFS), screening and management of anaemia, nutrition counselling
Sexual and reproductive health: counselling, contraceptive information and services, menstrual hygiene support
Mental health: counselling and support for stress, anxiety, and emotional well-being
Injuries and violence: counselling and support including for gender-based violence
Prevention of substance misuse: counselling and awareness on tobacco, alcohol and drugs
Non-communicable diseases: screening and lifestyle counselling
Adolescent Friendly Health Clinics (AFHC) at PHC, CHC and district hospital level providing confidential, free services
Peer Educator (Saathiya) programme — trained adolescent peers provide information at the community level
Adolescent Health Days and school/community outreach activities
Free routine check-ups and referral to higher facilities as needed
excluded_procedures: Services for individuals below 10 or above 19 years
Tertiary surgical/hospitalization care (referred to and covered under other schemes such as PM-JAY)
Diagnostics unrelated to the adolescent-health service package on a paid basis

required_documents.name: No document mandatory
required_documents.notes: Adolescents can access Adolescent Friendly Health Clinic services confidentially and free; no income or eligibility document is required. Services are confidential and youth-friendly.

required_documents.name: School ID / Aadhaar (optional)
required_documents.notes: Used only for record-keeping; absence of ID cannot be a reason to deny adolescent health services or counselling.

enrollment_channels: Adolescent Friendly Health Clinic (AFHC) at PHC, CHC, and district hospital
Government and government-aided schools (Adolescent Health programme / WIFS)
Peer Educators (Saathiya) at the community level
ASHA / ANM outreach and Adolescent Health Days
enrollment_steps: Step 1: No enrollment or fee needed — RKSK provides free, confidential services to all adolescents aged 10-19
Step 2: Adolescents can visit the nearest Adolescent Friendly Health Clinic (AFHC) for counselling and care
Step 3: In schools, adolescents receive Weekly Iron and Folic Acid Supplementation (WIFS) and health education
Step 4: Trained Peer Educators (Saathiya) provide information and refer adolescents to AFHCs at the community level
Step 5: Receive free counselling and services across nutrition, sexual/reproductive health, mental health, substance misuse, injuries/violence, and NCDs
Step 6: Complex cases are referred free of cost to higher government facilities
Step 7: Adolescent Health Days and outreach activities provide periodic services and awareness
description_for_embedding: Rashtriya Kishor Swasthya Karyakram (RKSK), the National Adolescent Health Programme launched by the Ministry of Health and Family Welfare on 7 January 2014 under the National Health Mission, provides free, confidential, youth-friendly health services to ALL adolescents aged 10 to 19 years — boys and girls, rural and urban, married and unmarried, in-school and out-of-school — regardless of family income, caste, religion, or BPL status, with special focus on marginalised and underserved groups. It expanded adolescent health beyond sexual and reproductive health to six priority areas: nutrition (Weekly Iron and Folic Acid Supplementation and anaemia management), sexual and reproductive health, mental health, injuries and violence (including gender-based violence), prevention of substance misuse, and non-communicable diseases. Services are delivered free through Adolescent Friendly Health Clinics (AFHCs) at PHC, CHC and district-hospital level, through the Peer Educator (Saathiya) programme where trained adolescents reach peers at the community level, and through schools, anganwadis and Adolescent Health Days. The package includes preventive, promotive, curative and counselling services with confidential routine check-ups and free referral to higher facilities. No enrollment fee and no mandatory documents are required, and services are confidential. Any adolescent between 10 and 19 — whether a school student, a working youth, or a young married girl — can walk in to an Adolescent Friendly Health Clinic or approach a Saathiya peer educator for free counselling, iron-folic-acid tablets for anaemia, and referral, without needing parental income proof or a BPL card.
